summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that the TCP stager endpoint serves implant binaries to any connecting client without authentication, allowing unauthenticated retrieval of sensitive payload data.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that an unauthenticated client can connect to the TCP stager port and retrieve the implant binary payload without providing any credentials or authentication.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully connected to the TCP stager listener and retrieved the test payload data without authentication. The verification confirmed that the TCP stager serves data to any client that connects.
verification_method: The test script starts a TCP listener serving a test payload, then connects as an unauthenticated client and verifies that the payload is received. Success is determined by matching the retrieved data against the expected proof string.
evidence_produced_by_run: Unauthenticated client successfully connected to TCP stager port
Test payload data was retrieved without providing any credentials
No authentication challenge or verification was observed during the connection
The TCP stager immediately sent data upon connection establishment

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the Sliver repository from GitHub on the host, checks out the latest commit, and builds the Docker image with the reproduction test.
